name: Argalario and Orconera Greenway Loop
name_original: Pista de La Orconera, Arroyo El Yedal — circular desde La Escontrilla
description: This difficult 6.9-mile (11.1 km) jogging route follows the historic Orconera mining railway, offering views of Santander and the Somorrostr
region_intro: Jogging routes around Valle de Trápaga-Trapagaran offer diverse landscapes shaped by its mining history and natural features. The region, located within the Triano mountain range, features transformed former iron ore mines now serving as artificial reservoirs and lakes, particularly around La Arboleda. Its terrain includes rugged mountain paths and unique karstic relief, providing varied running experiences. Best jogging routes around Valle De Trápaga-Trapagaran The most popular jogging route is Pista de La Orconera, Arroyo El Yedal — circular desde La Escontrilla, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This route explores the historic mining areas and offers scenic views from the hills. Another top favourite among local runners is Barrio Orconera, Matamoros, Pozo Parkotxa — circular desde San Gabriel, a difficult 6.4 miles (10.3 km) path. It features varied terrain through former mining landscapes and natural hills. Local runners also love the Ganeran Summit – Gasteran Peak loop from Zugaztieta, a 11.0 miles (17.7 km) trail leading through the Triano mountains, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Jogging around Valle De Trápaga-Trapagaran is defined by transformed mining areas, artificial reservoirs, and the rugged Triano mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging trails. The routes in Valle De Trápaga-Trapagaran are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 6 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Valle De Trápaga-Trapagaran's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I find in Valle De Trápaga-Trapagaran?
A: There are nearly 20 dedicated running routes available in Valle De Trápaga-Trapagaran, catering to various fitness levels. These trails explore the region's unique landscapes, from former mining areas to the rugged Triano mountains.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate jogging routes in the area?
A: Yes, while some routes are challenging, there are 11 moderate running routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38222487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Galindo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.7 km path that offers a less strenuous option for joggers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Valle De Trápaga-Trapagaran?
A: The terrain is quite varied, reflecting the region's unique history and geography. You'll find paths through transformed mining landscapes, around artificial reservoirs, and within the rugged Triano mountains. Some routes feature unique karstic relief, like 'El Laberinto' near Peñas Negras, offering diverse running experiences.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Valle De Trápaga-Trapagaran?
A: Yes, many routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1125949690/pista-de-la-orconera-arroyo-el-yedal-circular-desde-la-escontrilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pista de La Orconera, Arroyo El Yedal — circular desde La Escontrilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.1 km long and explores historic mining areas.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the jogging routes?
A: The routes often pass through areas rich in history and natural beauty. You can explore the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2939178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakes of the Old Iron Mines&lt;/a&gt;, which are former mining pits transformed into serene reservoirs. Higher elevations, accessible via the historic funicular railway, also offer panoramic views of Bilbao's El Abra port.

Q: Are the running trails in Valle De Trápaga-Trapagaran suitable for families?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not explicitly categorized, the presence of moderate trails and recreational areas like La Arboleda, with its walking and sports facilities, suggests that some paths could be suitable for families looking for lighter jogging or walks. Always check the route's difficulty and length before heading out with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: Generally, many outdoor trails in Spain are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is kept on a leash. However, it's always recommended to check local signage or specific route descriptions for any restrictions, particularly in protected natural areas or around wildlife habitats. Be sure to clean up after your pet.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: Yes, many trailheads, especially those starting from recreational hubs like La Arboleda or the Peñas Negras Visitor Center, offer parking facilities. For routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1125031789/barrio-orconera-matamoros-pozo-parkotxa-circular-desde-san-gabriel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrio Orconera, Matamoros, Pozo Parkotxa — circular desde San Gabriel&lt;/a&gt;, starting points often have nearby parking options, though availability can vary.

Q: How can I access the running routes using public transport?
A: Valle de Trápaga-Trapagaran is well-connected. The historic La Reineta funicular railway provides access to higher ground and various scenic paths, making it a unique way to reach certain trailheads. Additionally, local bus services connect the urban center ('Zona Baja') to different parts of the municipality, including areas closer to the trails.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Valle De Trápaga-Trapagaran?
A: The running routes in Valle De Trápaga-Trapagaran are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 6 reviews. Over 300 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unique blend of historical mining landscapes and natural beauty.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced trail runners?
A: Absolutely. The region offers 8 difficult routes for those seeking a challenge. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15051148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ganeran Summit – Gasteran Peak loop from Zugaztieta&lt;/a&gt; is an 17.7 km trail leading through the Triano mountains, involving significant elevation changes and taking over two hours to complete.

Q: Where can I find viewpoints with panoramic scenery?
A: From the higher elevations of the Triano mountains, particularly those accessible via the historic funicular railway, you can enjoy stunning panoramic views. These vantage points often overlook Bilbao's El Abra port and the surrounding diverse landscapes.
tips: It was declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in July 2014. It has two stops, Trapagaran and Larreineta. Originally its purpose was to transport goods to the mines located in the Triano mountains.
The effort is worth it, just to see the beautiful view.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Pista de La Orconera trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from La Escontrilla. You can typically find parking near the starting point in La Escontrilla, which is also home to the historic Funicular de La Escontrilla. Specific parking areas may vary, so it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Pista de La Orconera, Arroyo El Yedal trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and potential for wildlife or other trail users. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the trail's natural beauty.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging, with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early morning or late afternoon start is advisable. Winter jogging is possible, but be prepared for potentially wet or muddy sections, especially in areas with natural surfaces.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the Pista de La Orconera, Arroyo El Yedal circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Pista de La Orconera trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied and challenging, making it suitable for experienced joggers. You'll encounter sections of natural paths, paved and unpaved surfaces, and some asphalt. As it follows an old mining railway, there are six tunnels, some of which are motion-sensor lit. Be prepared for steep slopes in certain areas, reaching up to 12% grade, despite parts of the old railway line being relatively flat.

faqs.question: What interesting features or views will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a rich blend of history and natural beauty. You'll pass through historic mining tunnels and along the repurposed Orconera mining railway. Highlights include the Funicular de La Escontrilla, a Scenic Dirt Trail, and magnificent views of the Somorrostro Valley. A notable highlight is the View of Santander, which is well worth the effort. You'll also see pastures, farmhouses, and Atlantic vegetation, with former mining craters now forming tranquil lakes.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Vía Verde de la Orconera, Orconerako bidegorria, and Bidegorri de La Orconera. It also intersects with trails like [CIMA BI04] Argalario and passes through the Túnel de Montalbo.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, 11.1 km distance, 400 meters of elevation gain, and sections with steep slopes, this trail is generally not recommended for beginner joggers. It requires a good level of fitness and experience with varied terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or fees for jogging in Montes de Triano?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through an area historically associated with the Triano Mountains (Montes de Triano), there are no specific park entrance fees or unique jogging regulations beyond general trail etiquette. Always respect private property, stay on marked paths, and carry out anything you bring in.

faqs.question: How do I get to the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to La Escontrilla, near Ortuella, are available. It's best to check local bus or train schedules for routes serving Ortuella or nearby towns, as services can vary. From there, the trailhead at La Escontrilla should be accessible by a short walk.

faqs.question: Are there any rest areas or facilities along the Pista de La Orconera route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail features equipped rest areas that offer scenic views, particularly of the villages on the left bank of the estuary. These can be good spots to take a break, rehydrate, and enjoy the panorama.
